# OPTUM GX Documentation

- [OPTUM GX Documentation](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/index.md): OPTUM GX Documentation
- [OPTUM GX Python](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/index.md): Extend, automate, and customize your geotechnical workflows with Python in OPTUM GX.

## Materials

- [1 Notation](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/1-notation.md): In this section, a number of frequently used quantities are defined.
- [2 Elasticity](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/2-elasticity.md): For elastic materials, the strains and effective stresses are related in a one-to-one manner — that is, the strains generated through loading along one stress path will be recovered when unloading along the same stress path.
- [3 Hydraulic Models](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/3-hydraulic-models.md): Variably saturated flow through a deforming porous medium can be described by the mass balance equation:
- [4 Mohr-Coulomb](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/4-mohr-coulomb.md): The Mohr-Coulomb is the most basic model capable of reproducing the key features of soils. While more complex models in principle offer the possibility of
- [5 Tresca](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/5-tresca.md): The Tresca model is essentially a special case of the Mohr-Coulomb model where the cohesion is set equal to the undrained shear strength, c'=c_u, the
- [6 MC Engineering](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/6-mc-engineering.md): The Mohr-Coulomb and Tresca models both have their advantages and limitations. Under drained conditions, the Mohr-Coulomb model produces results that generally accepted and that have a transparent relation to the input parameters (E,\nu,c',\phi').
- [7 Drucker Prager](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/7-drucker-prager.md): The Drucker-Prager material is very similar to the Mohr-Coulomb material but uses slightly different expressions for the yield and plastic potential function.
- [8 Modified Cam Clay](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/materials/8-modified-cam-clay.md): The critical state models developed by Roscoe and his co-workers (Roscoe and Burland 1968; Schofield and Wroth 1968) in the 1960s have been widely applied in geomechanics and form the basis of many subsequent models.

## Python

### Introduction

- [Introduction](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/introduction/index.md): The OPTUM GX Python interface enables straightforward scripting control of geotechnical models, analyses, and results.
- [Generate Project Python Code](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/introduction/generate-project-python-code.md): Optum GX has a built-in feature to translate the input made in the user interface, directly to the corresponding Python code. This makes it easy to discover the functionality of python functions and convert your existing models to parametric python code.

### Setting Up IDE

- [Python Scripting with Spyder](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/setting-up-ide/spyder.md): Download and install the Spyder IDE from the official website: https://www.spyder-ide.org/download
- [Python Scripting with Visual Studio Code](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/setting-up-ide/vs-code.md): Download and install the Visual Studio Code (VS Code) from https://code.visualstudio.com

### Functions

#### Application

- [create_project](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/application/create_project.md): Create project.
- [get_current_project](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/application/get_current_project.md): Get current project.
- [open_project](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/application/open_project.md): Open GX project.
- [save_project](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/application/save_project.md): Save current project
- [write_step](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/application/write_step.md): Write step

#### Project

- [AUS](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/aus.md): Define an AUS material.
- [Beam](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/beam.md): Define a beam material
- [Connector](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/connector.md): Define a Connector material.
- [create_csys_2d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/create_csys_2d.md): Create two-dimensional coordinate system.
- [create_csys_3d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/create_csys_3d.md): Create three-dimensional coordinate system.
- [create_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/create_model.md): Create model in project
- [DruckerPrager](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/druckerprager.md): Define a Drucker-Prager material.
- [FlatPlateConcrete](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/flatplateconcrete.md): Define a Flat Plate Concrete material.
- [GeneralPlate](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/generalplate.md): Define a General Plate material.
- [Geogrid](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/geogrid.md): Define a Geogrid material
- [get_csys](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/get_csys.md): Obtains a list of coordinate systems.
- [get_current_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/get_current_model.md): Get current model.
- [get_current_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/get_current_stage.md): Get current stage.
- [get_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/get_model.md): Get model or models by name.
- [HardeningSoil](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/hardeningsoil.md): Define a Hardeing Soil material.
- [HMC](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/hmc.md): Define a Hardening Mohr-Coulomb material.
- [HoekBrown](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/hoekbrown.md): Define a Hoek-Brown material.
- [LinearElastic](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/linearelastic.md): Define a Linear Elastic material.
- [ModifiedCamClay](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/modifiedcamclay.md): Define a Modified Cam Clay material.
- [MohrCoulomb](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/mohrcoulomb.md): Define a Mohr-Coulomb material.
- [MohrCoulombEngineering](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/mohrcoulombengineering.md): Define a Mohr-Coulomb Engineering material.
- [MultiMohrCoulomb](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/multimohrcoulomb.md): Define a Multi Mohr-Coulomb material.
- [NailRow](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/nailrow.md): Define a Nail Row material.
- [NGIADP](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/ngiadp.md): Define a NGI-ADP material.
- [PileRow](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/pilerow.md): Define a Pile Row material.
- [Rigid](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/rigid.md): Define a Rigid material.
- [RigidPlate](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/rigidplate.md): Define a Rigid Plate material.
- [run_analysis](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/run_analysis.md): Run analysis
- [set_current_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/set_current_model.md): Sets the current model.
- [SheetPile](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/sheetpile.md): Define a Sheet Pile material.
- [Tresca](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/tresca.md): Define a Tresca material.
- [Water](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/project/water.md): Define Water material.

#### Model

- [add_arc](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_arc.md): Draw an arc in 2D, using N line segments between two points and a midpoint.
- [add_box](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_box.md): Create a solid box in 3D between a start and end point.
- [add_circle](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_circle.md): Draw a circle in 2D, from center point and radius, using N line segments.
- [add_connector](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_connector.md): add connector
- [add_line](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_line.md): Add a line defined by two points.
- [add_nprism](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_nprism.md): Draw a Nprism in 3D.
- [add_polygon](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_polygon.md): Add a polygon defined by a two-dimensional array of vertex points.
- [add_polygons](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_polygons.md): Add a list of polygons defined by an array of two-dimensional arrays of vertex points for each polygon.
- [add_prism](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_prism.md): Draw a prism in 3D.
- [add_rectangle](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_rectangle.md): Create a rectangle in 2D between a start and end point.
- [add_sphere](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_sphere.md): Add a sphere from center point and radius, using N line segments.
- [add_vertex](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/add_vertex.md): Create a point in 2D or 3D.
- [clone](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/clone.md): Clones the selected model.
- [create_3d_from_2d_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/create_3d_from_2d_model.md): Extrudes a 2D model to 3D.
- [create_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/create_stage.md): Creates a new stage.
- [del_shapes](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/del_shapes.md): Delete shapes and their definition.
- [delete](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/delete.md): Delete model or stage.
- [delete_interior](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/delete_interior.md): Delete shapes and leave voids in their place.
- [edges](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/edges.md): Get all edges in model.
- [extrude](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/extrude.md): Extrude a shape.
- [faces](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/faces.md): Get all faces in model.
- [get_active_csys](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_active_csys.md): Obtains the active coordinate system.
- [get_current_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_current_stage.md): Obtain current stage.
- [get_features](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_features.md): Obtain features associated with shapes.
- [get_selected_shapes](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_selected_shapes.md): Return selected shapes in GUI
- [get_shape_by_id](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_shape_by_id.md): Get shape by id.
- [get_shapes](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_shapes.md): Get shapes in a model.
- [get_shapes_by_vertices](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_shapes_by_vertices.md): Get shapes bounded by vertices.
- [get_shared_edges](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_shared_edges.md): Obtain the shared edges between two lists of shapes.
- [get_shared_faces](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_shared_faces.md): Obtain the shared faces between two lists of shapes.
- [get_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_stage.md): Obtain a list of stages.
- [get_sub_shapes](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_sub_shapes.md): Get subshapes in a model.
- [get_vertices](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/get_vertices.md): Get vertex from their coordinates.
- [mirror](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/mirror.md): Mirror shapes.
- [move](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/move.md): Move shapes.
- [remove_features](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/remove_features.md): Remove features
- [revolve](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/revolve.md): Revolve shape around axis.
- [revolve_2d_to_3d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/revolve_2d_to_3d.md): revolve 2D model to 3D.
- [rotate](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/rotate.md): rotate shapes.
- [scale](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/scale.md): Scale shapes.
- [select](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/select.md): Select geometric objects by point or bounding box.
- [Set plate](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_plate.md): Set shape material as plate.
- [set_active_csys](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_active_csys.md): Set the active coordinate system.
- [set_body_load](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_body_load.md): Add body load.
- [set_current_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_current_stage.md): Sets the current stage.
- [set_fixed_excess_pressure](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_fixed_excess_pressure.md): Set shape to fixed excess pressure.
- [set_fixed_head](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_fixed_head.md): Set shape to fixed head.
- [set_fixed_pressure](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_fixed_pressure.md): Set shape to fixed pressure.
- [set_fixedendanchor](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_fixedendanchor.md): Set fixed-end anchor from vertex.
- [set_from_model](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_from_model.md): Set model from another model.
- [set_geogrid](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_geogrid.md): Set shape as geogrid
- [set_hinge_2d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_hinge_2d.md): Set hinge in 2D.
- [set_hinge_3d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_hinge_3d.md): Set hinge in 3D.
- [set_interface](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_interface.md): Define interface.
- [set_line_load](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_line_load.md): Add line load.
- [set_line_moment](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_line_moment.md): Add line moment.
- [set_mesh_fan](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_mesh_fan.md): Set mesh fan around vertex
- [set_mesh_size](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_mesh_size.md): Set mesh size to shape
- [set_nailrow](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_nailrow.md): Set shape as nail row.
- [set_no_flow](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_no_flow.md): Set shape to disallow flow
- [set_plate_bc](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_plate_bc.md): Set plate boundary condition.
- [set_point_bc](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_point_bc.md): Set point boundary condition.
- [set_point_load](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_point_load.md): Add point load.
- [set_prestress](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_prestress.md): Set prestress.
- [set_resultpoint](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_resultpoint.md): Set points to record results.
- [set_run_flag](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_run_flag.md): Set run flag for model or stage.
- [set_seepage_face](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_seepage_face.md): Set shape to allow seepage
- [set_solid](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_solid.md): Set shape material as solid.
- [set_solver_settings](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_solver_settings.md): Define solver settings.
- [set_standard_fixities](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_standard_fixities.md): Add standard support conditions to model.
- [set_support](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_support.md): Set support conditions to shapes
- [set_surface_load](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_surface_load.md): Add surface load.
- [set_vertex_position](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_vertex_position.md): Set new postion of vertex.
- [set_water_table](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/set_water_table.md): Define watertable at shape.
- [slice_3d_to_2d](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/slice_3d_to_2d.md): Slide a 3D model to 2D.
- [vertices](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/vertices.md): Get all vertices in model.
- [volumes](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/volumes.md): Get all volumes in model.
- [zoom_all](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/model/zoom_all.md): Zoom to view all geometry.

#### Stage

- [get_analysis_properties](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/stage/get_analysis_properties.md): Get analysis properties.
- [set_analysis_properties](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/stage/set_analysis_properties.md): Define analysis properties
- [set_from_stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/stage/set_from_stage.md): Set stage from another stage in the same model.
- [stage](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/stage/set_run_flag.md): Set run flag for model or stage.
- [toggle_features](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/stage/toggle_features.md): Toggle features for stage

#### Utilities

- [Utilities](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/utilities/index.md)
- [Run OptumGX from Python](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/functions/utilities/run-gx-from-python.md): This article outlines a helper function that checks if an instance of OptumGX is running. If not, the application is started. The input parameters are set to the default, but may need to be altered to match the current machine.

### Examples

- [Examples](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/examples/index.md)
- [1. Shallow foundation](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/examples/example1_shallow_foundation.md): This example deals with the an eccentrically loaded foundation. The soil is saturated clay and the analysis is to be performed assuming undrained conditions.
- [3.  Settlement of strip footing on elastic soil](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/examples/example3_strip_footing.md): This example deals with a centrally loaded weightless rigid strip footing on an elastic soil. The footing and the soil are modeled using the default materials Rigid and Linear Elastic, respectively.
- [6. Slope stability - Long term](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/examples/example6_slope_stability_long_term.md): In this example we consider the stability of a layered slope.
- [10. Slope with pre-existing fault](https://docs.optumce.com/optum-gx/python/examples/example10_slope_fault.md): In this example we consider the stability of slope with, and without, a fault line.
